items.title: Telstra's returning CEO Vicki Brady 'deeply sorry' for national outage

children.content: Telstra chief executive Vicki Brady apologises to the company's customers, saying the telco let them down and that investigations into the cause of Wednesday's Telstra outage are continuing.

articles.title: Telstra should 'face the music' over outage, minister says

children.content: Communications Minister Anika Wells says Telstra should "face the music" over the nationwide outage earlier this week. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Annika Wells in white in a side profile in front of Australian flag.

articles.title: Telstra shares new details of possible outage death

children.content: Telstra says it has no record of Triple Zero (000) calls made from phone numbers associated with the address of a person who later died in a South Australian regional hospital on Wednesday.

image.altText: A middle-aged man with glasses speaks into a microphone in front of a blue Telstra backdrop.

articles.title: Government, experts repeatedly warned of issue that caused Telstra outage

children.content: Government agencies and academics both recently warned Telstra it was vulnerable to the type of error that caused this week's national outage.

image.altText: A logo on the side of a Telstra building

gallery.altText: Brady stands at a lectern, looking down

thumbnail.altText: Brady stands at a lectern, looking down

items.title: Derryn Hinch, 'human headline' and media figure, dies aged 82

image.altText: Hinch, dressed in a red scarf, talks to reporters outside Parliament House in Canberra

children.content: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders, which earned him several stints in jail.

items.title: X under fire for 'shocking disrespect' of antisemitism royal commission

children.content: Social media giants, including Elon Musk's X Corp, have come under fire with the royal commission on antisemitism expressing frustration at platforms' refusal to engage.

image.altText: Elon Musk pouts his lips in a close up portrait

items.title: H5 bird flu strain detected in Australian seabird for first time

children.content: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

image.altText: Two hands holding two canisters with yellow lids and red liquid

items.title: Trump tariff fight resumes as Australia tells US it has no evidence

children.content: The Australian embassy in Washington lodges an objection to the Trump administration's proposal for a 12.5 per cent tariff on Australian imports.

image.altText: Donald Trump sits at a desk holding a black folder open with yellow-gold curtains and two flags behind him.

items.title: Police slide into DMs of underworld group chat with warning

children.content: An encrypted phone network allegedly used by Sydney's underworld to orchestrate murders, kidnappings and drug deals has been infiltrated by NSW Police after they arrested a trio accused of running the "VIP Network".

image.altText: Photo graphic of NSW Police message to underworld device, top right, and officers, left side

items.title: Iran targets US military sites in Gulf as Israel warns it could 'strike again'

children.content: Iranian armed forces launched reciprocal attacks on US military infrastructure in Gulf states following US strikes on Iran's south and east regions, straining a three-week-old ceasefire.

articles.title: Not only is the Iran war not over, it is at a dangerous inflection point

children.content: Not only is the war not over, it is once again at a dangerous inflection point, as the US and Iran revert to military options to deal with differences over their interim peace agreement.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks in front of a US flag.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

articles.title: As the ceasefire cracks, Iran gets 'a complete reset' of pressure on its oil trade

children.content: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran is fraying and Washington has attempted to crush the regime's oil sales, but Tehran made its move when the blockade lifted weeks ago.

image.altText: A gas flare on an oil production platform in the Soroush oil fields is seen alongside an Iranian flag in the Persian Gulf, Iran

gallery.altText: Burning debris near a shipping port

thumbnail.altText: Burning debris near a shipping port

items.title: The 'stunt' that could cost UK far right leader Nigel Farage his political career

children.content: Nigel Farage is facing intensifying scrutiny over his finances and millions of dollars' worth of donations. Here's how he got here.

image.altText: A man in a dark suit and pale lavender tie smiles while holding a beer outside a British pub.

items.title: Wildfire in southern Spain kills 12 as heatwave continues

children.content: A fast-moving wildfire in southern Spain kills 12 as a heatwave pushes temperatures up over much of the country.

image.altText: Firefighters in hi-vis spray water on a wildfire in a national park.

items.title: The meteorite at the centre of a 'culture war' between the UK and Australia

children.content: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

image.altText: A meteorite with a tail of flame burning behind it hits the Earth with a purple sky above.

articles.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

articles.title: Indigenous-led whale tour shares thousands of years of observations and knowledge

children.content: A new Indigenous-led whale-watching cruise is aiming to give an intimate insight into First Nations people's deep historical connection with the majestic mammals.

image.altText: Two large whales breach over the water and are captured mid air.

gallery.altText: A meteorite travels through deep space lit up from the inside.

thumbnail.altText: A meteorite travels through deep space lit up from the inside.

items.title: Inside the alleged plot to kill Trump and Musk at UFC White House event

children.content: Eight men have been accused of planning a drone and shooting attack that aimed to kill the US president and SpaceX founder at the UFC Freedom 250 event held at the White House in June.

image.altText: A canopy over a UFC ring in the dark sky with the White House in the background

items.title: Charlie Kirk's family requests evidence against accused killer be released

children.content: The family of conservative activist Charlie Kirk has pleaded with a judge to allow more public access to evidence presented against the man accused of the assassination.

image.altText: A rear view of a Robinson wearing a white button-up shirt sitting in a courtroom.

items.title: Police find human remains during search for missing teen

children.content: Detectives are investigating after human remains were found during a search for a missing teenage girl in Victoria's west.

image.altText: police pic of layla

items.title: Cancelled musician loses fight against orchestra over political beliefs

children.content: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, 39, bearded, in a suit, including black tie, walks outside a court.

items.title: The Rolling Stones's vigorous 25th album proves age is but a number

children.content: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

gallery.altText: The Rolling Stones's Mick Jagger, Keith Richards and Ronnie Wood pose and smile together.

thumbnail.altText: The Rolling Stones's Mick Jagger, Keith Richards and Ronnie Wood pose and smile together.

items.title: How to choose the right potato for any meal you're cooking

children.content: Potatoes are undeniably versatile and tasty but choosing which variety to buy has never been more complicated. Brushed or washed? Yellow, white or pink? Mashing or roasting? And now there are low-carb spuds too.

image.altText: Roasted hasselback potatoes sprinkled with rosemary and other spices, in a round dish lined with baking paper.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Red cards, ballistic missiles and Pam the Bird

children.content: It's been a week of missile testing, Telstra outages and FIFA World Cup red card interventions. Were you paying attention?

image.altText: A white cartoon bird with an orange beak spraypainted sideways down a concrete tower.

items.title: As a pup, authorities saved Neil the seal from drowning. Now, he's here to stay

children.content: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving behind a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.

gallery.altText: A young elephant seal behind a ute tray.
Juvenile elephant seal with its mother.
An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.

An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.

items.title: Twelve Apostles Visitors Centre worksite shut down after man loses hand

children.content: A workman has lost his hand in a workplace accident while cleaning out a sewer pit along the Great Ocean Road this morning.

image.altText: Tourists stand in gloomy weather staring out to sea at the 12 Apostles.

items.title: Workers' pay has not kept pace with productivity growth in 30 years

children.content: Research suggests that the typical worker's pay has not kept pace with productivity growth in Australia for 30 years.

image.altText: people, workers at lunctime

items.title: PM to unveil AI agenda as government seeks to calm copyright concerns

children.content: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

image.altText: A man speaks at a lectern.

items.title: Brisbane Airport flight paths altered following noise review

children.content: People living in areas such as Redcliffe, southern Moreton Bay, Bribie Island and Logan are set to benefit the most, but there will be no change to the flight path over Bulimba.

image.altText: A qantas plane taking off into a clear blue sky with a city skyline at the bottom in the distance

items.title: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win World Cup in any fashion

children.content: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

articles.title: Quick hits: It's the Mbappé show again, as England defender cops ban

children.content: An England defender pays the price for a terrible red-card tackle, and it's the Mbappé show in Boston as France advances. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Chemsdine Chalbi, Kylian Mbappe, Jarell Quansah

articles.title: France's superstars step up to beat Morocco and reach World Cup semis

children.content: Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé deliver France a comprehensive 2-0 win over Morocco to send Les Bleus into the World Cup semifinals.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappe clenches his fists and smiles while looking at the camera

gallery.altText: Kylian Mbappe has his arms outstretched and a triumphant look on his face after scoring

thumbnail.altText: Kylian Mbappe has his arms outstretched and a triumphant look on his face after scoring

items.title: Gauff's horror miss sets up historic all-Czech Wimbledon women's final

children.content: Coco Gauff is left to rue a regulation miss on match point as Karolina Muchová and Linda Nosková set up an all-Czech date at Wimbledon.

image.altText: Coco Gauff scratches her face with her left hand in disappointment

items.title: Big Bash to play historic season-opener in India

children.content: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

image.altText: Nick Hobson of the Scorchers and Aaron Hardie of the Scorchers pose with the BBL trophy

items.title: Pogačar's daring knows no bounds as he crushes Tour rivals

children.content: Tadej Pogačar delivers another masterclass in grand tour dominance, conquering the Col du Tourmalet and crushing his Tour de France rivals with a daring attack.

image.altText: Tadej Pogacar bows over the line

items.title: McKeown 'gutted' as illness forces Commonwealth Games withdrawal

children.content: Kaylee McKeown withdraws from the Commonwealth Games after glandular fever causes shortness of breath and a loss of appetite.

image.altText: Kaylee McKeown looks to her left at the wall after a swimming race in Brisbane in 2025.

items.title: Cattle farmer plants 14,000 trees to help slow flow of flood water

children.content: Jeff Imeson has planted around 14,000 trees on his property near Nimbin in a bid to help slow the flow of future floods.

Bird eye view shot of farm land showing lots of new plantings on Jeff Imeson's property in the Northern Rivers
A man and a woman look out over a creek bank at new tree planting to help reduce flood damage

items.title: WA traditional owners say they are being ignored amid developments

children.content: Traditional owners from WA's Wheatbelt say a historic native title settlement has increased division among Noongar groups.

image.altText: Three men stand talking to each other on a large rocky surface high above bushland under a cloudy, sunny sky.

items.title: How China's military goals in the Pacific are rapidly progressing

children.content: China's firing of a dummy warhead was the first time in two years it tested its long-range missile arsenal — and the first time ever it had launched such a rocket from a nuclear-powered submarine.

image.altText: A rocket launches from the sea leaving smoke in its wake.

items.title: Looking back at Bonnie Tyler's impact on pop culture

children.content: Tyler's music continuously found newer, younger audiences thanks to it soundtracking memorable scenes in movies throughout the decades.

thumbnail.altText: An animation of Shrek 2 character Fairy Godmother singing on stage in a red dress

items.title: Sydney man demanded underage girl work 12-hour days at brothels

children.content: A Sydney judge found 45-year-old Surya Subekti has demonstrated no genuine remorse.

image.altText: AFP officers arresting a man.

items.title: Mail sorter charged after suspected theft of Australia Post packages

children.content: A 57-year-old Australia Post worker is charged by police with mail tampering at a sorting facility in Perth, with $34,000 seized during a search of his home and car.

image.altText: Two AFP officers escort a man wearing a dark hoodie jumper down the driveway of a property with their backs turned.

items.title: Six-year-old's love for garbage trucks earns him 'work' on bin rounds

children.content: Perth boy Thomas Aldworth has been welcomed as an official helper by the City of Perth waste management team and he even has his own uniform.
